mission.title: node-exporter: node_exporter 1.5.0 release SIGKILL immediately on macOS M1/arm64
mission.description: node_exporter 1.5.0 release SIGKILL immediately on macOS M1/arm64. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current node-exporter deployment
steps.description:
Verify your node-exporter version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
helm list -n node-exporter 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working node-exporter installation.

steps.title: Review node-exporter configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant node-exporter configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get configmap -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=node-exporter
```
### Host operating system: output of `uname -a`

```
$ uname -a
Darwin Peters-MBP-2.cust.communityfibre.co.uk 21.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Mon Aug 22 2052 PDT 2022; root:xnu-8020.140.49~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64
```

### node_exporter

steps.title: Apply the fix for node_exporter 1.5.0 release SIGKILL immediately on macOS…
steps.description:
Is this the same issue as #2217?

There is something node exporter specific going on here. Comparing with the statsd exporter (released both before and after node exporter 1.5.0):

```terminal
❯ codesign -vvv node_exporter
node_exporter: valid on disk
node_exporter: satisfies its Designated
```yaml
$ uname -a
Darwin Peters-MBP-2.cust.communityfibre.co.uk 21.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Mon Aug 22 20:19:52 PDT 2022; root:xnu-8020.140.49~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get events -n node-exporter --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "node_exporter 1.5.0 release SIGKILL immediately on macOS…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Is this the same issue as #2217?

There is something node exporter specific going on here.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with node-exporter installed or the issue environment reproducible.
